game_name: Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each character has unique secondary (grenades for Han, spinning attack for Chewbacca)
Character switching integrated into story progression
Unlocked progression reward for Luke character
AT-AT walking sequence reportedly striking visual set-piece

typical_relief_triggers: Password save system (allows breaks between sessions)
Character ability variety (different tools for different threats)
typical_tension_triggers: Blind jumps without visible landing zones
Large number of enemies on screen simultaneously
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Relentless action with escalating challenge; reviewers noted hard difficulty, frustrating enemy placement, and dense enemy counts creating sustained tension rather than rhythmic cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core platforming and combat mechanics; manageable threat density
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Secondary weapons and character-specific abilities; increasing enemy complexity and on-screen count
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mode 7 vehicle sequences and multiple boss encounters; Darth Vader final boss; Force Powers available for Luke
notable_spikes: Off-screen enemy spawning and cheap hits cited as difficulty spikes
Blind jumps without clear visual landing feedback
Density of simultaneous enemies described as overwhelming

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-game checkpoint that unlocks Force Powers for Luke and transitions to enhanced abilities; story-driven progression gate
Quasi-3D vehicle-based level using Mode 7 effects; described as 'absolutely stunning' set-piece with cinematic presentation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ice Fields of Hoth AT-AT attack

notable_level_patterns.description: Platforming sections requiring jumps to invisible or off-screen platforms; sources criticize as frustrating design
